explanation: The code defines a Node structure that contains a Person object and pointers to both the next and previous nodes, allowing for bidirectional traversal of the list. The List class encompasses basic functionalities such as insertion (at both the front and back), removal (from both ends), checks for emptiness, size counting, searching for a specific person, and sorting the list. It also includes methods to access the first and last elements of the list and ensures proper memory management through its destructor.
name: 2007-05-07_c
subject: C
summary: This project implements a doubly linked list data structure in C++, designed to manage objects of the Person class.
